title: myosotis arvensis var. grandiflora
intro: Myosotis arvensis var. grandiflora, also known as large-flowered field forget-me-not, is a flowering plant that belongs to the borage family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which offer health benefits such as relieving congestion and promoting relaxation. Additionally, the plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat conditions like fever, skin irritations, and digestive issues.
